weatherForecast.comment: Above around 1500 metres, and generally in the northern Niedere Tauern, the first half of the night will be cloudless. After that, the clouds will increase from the south-west. Clouds will generally dominate Tuesday, with only a brief southerly wind around noon to soften the weather. While the peaks of the northern Niedere Tauern will remain mostly clear, visibility on the southern side of the Alps will deteriorate from midday. From the late afternoon it will start to snow lightly from the Gurktal Alps via the Seetal Alps to the Koralm, with rain falling below 1400 metres. The temperature at an altitude of 2000 metres will be between -8 and -1 degrees. The temperature at an altitude of 1500 metres is between -5 and 1 degree. Moderate to strong winds with gusts of around 60 km/h from the south-west. Up to 80 km/h are possible on the Koralm.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Persistent weak layer problem! Take particular care in gullies and bowls in northern and eastern exposures!
avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche risk is assessed as moderate above the tree line and low below. The snow layering is still very weak, especially in the northern and eastern sectors. Here, avalanches can be triggered by even small additional loads and can reach medium size when breaking through the weak old snowpack. In some cases, there is a thin, bound layer from the last snowfall. The danger areas are mainly located in the transition from little to more snow as well as behind terrain edges and in the entrance area of gullies and bowls. Some of the avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.

snowpackStructure.comment: The total snow depth is well below average. A thin, partly bound snowpack covers a very variable and mostly hard old snowpack. There is significantly more snow in shady gullies and bowls than on sunny slopes, which are partly bare up to 2000 metres. In the northern and eastern exposures, the snowpack is still weakened by layers with faceted crystals and floating snow (persistent weak layer problem!).

tendency.highlights: An Italian low will bring snowfall and rain on Wednesday. The snowfall level will be between 800 and 1500 metres above sea level. There will be hardly any precipitation on the northern side of the Alps. The southerly wind will ease in the west. The avalanche risk increases slightly with the new fallen snow.

weatherForecast.comment: There will be a lot of clouds in the peripheral mountains at night, from which scattered snowflakes may fall. Clouds will increase from the south-west on Tuesday. This means that higher peaks will also be covered in cloud. Visibility will be poor and it may snow lightly at times. The snowfall level will be between the valleys and around 1000 metres above sea level. The temperature at an altitude of 1500 metres is between -5 and -2 degrees. At an altitude of 1000 metres, temperatures will be between -3 and 0 degrees. Moderate to partly brisk winds with gusts of around 40 km/h from the southwest, even stronger in exposed areas.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Low avalanche danger with mostly little snow.
avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche risk is low. In extremely steep gullies or bowls on shady slopes, the triggering of small slab avalanches cannot be completely ruled out.

snowpackStructure.comment: There is still significantly less snow than average. A thin, partly bound snowpack covers a very variable and mostly hard old snowpack. In shady gullies and bowls, the connection between the bound snowpack and the old snowpack is sometimes poor. In addition, angular shapes can weaken the snow base, fundament.

tendency.highlights: An Italian low will bring snowfall and rain on Wednesday. The snowfall level will be between 800 and 1500 metres above sea level. The southerly wind is easing. With the new fallen snow, the avalanche risk increases slightly on Wednesday afternoon.

weatherForecast.comment: In the northern Alps and generally at altitudes above 1500 metres, the night will be cloudless. Clouds will gather from the south on Tuesday. While the peaks of the western Northern Alps will remain fog-free, visibility will deteriorate from Hochschwab to Veitsch. However, there will be no precipitation. The temperature at an altitude of 2000 metres will be between -8 and -1 degrees. The temperature at an altitude of 1500 metres will be between -4 and 2 degrees. Brisk to stormy winds with gusts between 40 and 60 km/h from south to south-west.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Low avalanche danger, but persistent weak layer problems in the northern and eastern exposures!
avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche risk is low. In the northern and eastern exposures, there is still a persistent weak layer problem at altitudes above the tree line. In steep terrain, small slab avalanches can be triggered in a few places by individual winter sports enthusiasts. Avalanche prone locations are mainly at the entrance to on shady slopes and bowls as well as behind terrain edges. In general, the risk of being swept away, falling and injury outweighs the risk of burial. However, be careful in the area of terrain traps.

snowpackStructure.comment: There is still significantly less snow than average. A thin, partly bound snowpack covers a very variable and mostly hard old snowpack. There is significantly more snow in shady gullies and bowls than on sunny slopes, some of which are partly bare up to 2000 metres. In the northern and eastern exposures, the snowpack is still weakened by layers with faceted crystals and floating snow (persistent weak layer problem!).

tendency.highlights: An Italian low will bring snowfall and rain to the south on Wednesday. The snowfall level will be between 800 and 1500 metres above sea level. There will be hardly any precipitation on the northern side of the Alps. The southerly wind will ease in the west. No change in the avalanche danger is expected.

ALBINA.generalHeadline: Note the persistent weak layer problem in the northern and eastern exposures!
